AcceptConnectionProposal.name: AcceptConnectionProposal
AcceptConnectionProposal.documentation:
Accepts a connection proposal which was generated at a supported partner's portal.

The proposal contains the Environment and bandwidth that were chosen on the partner's portal and cannot be modified.

Upon accepting the proposal a connection will be made between the AWS network as accessed via the selected Attach Point and the network previously selected network on the partner's portal.

CreateConnection.name: CreateConnection
CreateConnection.documentation:
Initiates the process to create a Connection across the specified Environment.

The Environment dictates the specified partner and location to which the other end of the connection should attach. You can see a list of the available Environments by calling ListEnvironments

The Attach Point specifies where within the AWS Network your connection will logically connect.

After a successful call to this method, the resulting Connection will return an Activation Key which will need to be brought to the specific partner's portal to confirm the Connection on both sides. (See Environment$activationPageUrl for a direct link to the partner portal).

DeleteConnection.name: DeleteConnection
DeleteConnection.documentation:
Deletes an existing Connection with the supplied identifier.

This operation will also inform the remote partner of your intention to delete your connection. Note, the partner may still require you to delete to fully clean up resources, but the network connectivity provided by the Connection will cease to exist.

DescribeConnectionProposal.name: DescribeConnectionProposal
DescribeConnectionProposal.documentation: Describes the details of a connection proposal generated at a partner's portal.

GetConnection.name: GetConnection
GetConnection.documentation: Describes the current state of a Connection resource as specified by the identifier.

ListAttachPoints.name: ListAttachPoints
ListAttachPoints.documentation: Lists all Attach Points the caller has access to that are valid for the specified Environment.

ListConnections.name: ListConnections
ListConnections.documentation:
Lists all connection objects to which the caller has access.

Allows for optional filtering by the following properties:

  • state

  • environmentId

  • provider

  • attach point

Only Connection objects matching all filters will be returned.

ListEnvironments.name: ListEnvironments
ListEnvironments.documentation: Lists all of the environments that can produce connections that will land in the called AWS region.

ListTagsForResource.name: ListTagsForResource
ListTagsForResource.documentation: List all current tags on the specified resource. Currently this supports Connection resources.

TagResource.name: TagResource
TagResource.documentation: Add new tags to the specified resource.

UpdateConnection.name: UpdateConnection
UpdateConnection.documentation: Modifies an existing connection. Currently we support modifications to the connection's description and/or bandwidth.

attachPoint.documentation: The Attach Point to which the connection should be associated.

activationKey.documentation:
An Activation Key that was generated on a supported partner's portal. This key captures the desired parameters from the initial creation request.

The details of this request can be described using with DescribeConnectionProposal.

tags.documentation: The tags to associate with the resulting Connection.

AccessDeniedException.documentation: The calling principal is not allowed to access the specified resource, or the resource does not exist.

directConnectGateway.documentation: Identifies an DirectConnect Gateway attach point by DirectConnectGatewayID.

arn.documentation: Identifies an attach point by full ARN.

AttachPoint.documentation: Identifies an attach point to use with a Connection.

type.documentation:
The type of this AttachPoint, which will dictate the syntax of the identifier.

Current types include:

  • ARN

  • DirectConnect Gateway

identifier.documentation: The identifier for the specific type of the AttachPoint.

name.documentation: The descriptive name of the identifier attach point.

AttachPointDescriptor.documentation: Describes a possible Attach Point for a Connection.

supported.documentation: The list of all bandwidths that this environment plans to support

Bandwidths.documentation: Contains the details about the available and supported bandwidths.

id.documentation: The short identifier of the connection object.

bandwidth.documentation: The specific selected bandwidth of this connection.

attachPoint.documentation: The Attach Point to which the connection should be associated."

environmentId.documentation: The specific Environment this connection is placed upon.

provider.documentation: The provider on the remote side of this Connection.

location.documentation: The provider specific location on the remote side of this Connection

state.documentation:
• requested: The initial state of a connection. The state will remain here until the Connection is accepted on the Partner portal.

  • pending: The connection has been accepted and is being provisioned between AWS and the Partner.

  • available: The connection has been fully provisioned between AWS and the Partner.

  • deleting: The connection is being deleted.

  • deleted: The connection has been deleted.

  • failed: The connection has failed to be created.

  • updating: The connection is being updated.

sharedId.documentation: An identifier used by both AWS and the remote partner to identify the specific connection.

billingTier.documentation: The billing tier this connection is currently assigned.

activationKey.documentation: The Activation Key associated to this connection.

Connection.documentation: The object describing the provided connectivity from the AWS region to the partner location.

attachPoint.documentation: The Attach Point to which the connection should be associated.

provider.documentation: The provider on the remote end of this Connection

location.documentation: The provider specific location at the remote end of this Connection

type.documentation: The product variant supplied by this resource.

state.documentation:
• requested: The initial state of a connection. The state will remain here until the Connection is accepted on the Partner portal.

  • pending: The connection has been accepted and is being provisioned between AWS and the Partner.

  • available: The connection has been fully provisioned between AWS and the Partner.

  • deleting: The connection is being deleted.

  • deleted: The connection has been deleted.

  • failed: The connection has failed to be created.

  • updating: The connection is being updated.

sharedId.documentation: An identifier used by both AWS and the remote partner to identify the specific connection.

billingTier.documentation: The billing tier this connection is currently assigned.

attachPoint.documentation: The Attach Point to which the connection should be associated."

environmentId.documentation:
The identifier of the Environment across which this Connection should be created.

The available Environment objects can be determined using ListEnvironments.

remoteAccount.documentation: Account and/or principal identifying information that can be verified by the partner of this specific Environment.

tags.documentation: The tag to associate with the resulting Connection.

identifier.documentation: The identifier of the Connection to be deleted.

connection.documentation: The Connection object that has been marked for deletion.

activationKey.documentation: An Activation Key that was generated on a supported partner's portal. This key captures the desired parameters from the initial creation request.

environmentId.documentation: The identifier of the Environment upon which the Connection would be placed if this proposal were accepted.

provider.documentation: The partner provider of the specific Environment of the proposal.

location.documentation: The partner specific location distinguisher of the specific Environment of the proposal.

provider.documentation: The provider on the remote side of this Connection.

location.documentation: The provider specific location on the remote side of this Connection.

state.documentation:
The state of the Environment. Possible values:

  • available: The environment is available and new Connection objects can be requested.

  • limited: The environment is available, but overall capacity is limited. The set of available bandwidths

  • unavailable: The environment is currently unavailable.

bandwidths.documentation: The sets of bandwidths that are available and supported on this environment.

type.documentation: The specific product type of Connection objects provided by this Environment.

activationPageUrl.documentation: An HTTPS URL on the remote partner portal where the Activation Key should be brought to complete the creation process.

remoteIdentifierType.documentation: The type of identifying information that should be supplied to the remoteAccount parameter of a CreateConnection call for this specific Environment.

Environment.documentation:
Defines the logical topology that an AWS Interconnect Connection is created upon.

Specifically, an Environment defines the partner The remote Cloud Service Provider of this resource. or The remote Last Mile Provider of this resource. and the region or location specification to which an AWS Interconnect Connection can be made.

id.documentation: The identifier of the specific Environment to describe.

InterconnectClientException.documentation: The request was denied due to incorrect client supplied parameters.

InterconnectServerException.documentation: The request resulted in an exception internal to the service.

InterconnectValidationException.documentation: The input fails to satisfy the constraints specified.

environmentId.documentation: The identifier of the Environment for which to list valid Attach Points.

maxResults.documentation: The max number of list results in a single paginated response.

nextToken.documentation: A pagination token from a previous paginated response indicating you wish to get the next page.
A pagination token indicating that there are more results that can be fetched.

maxResults.documentation: The max number of list results in a single paginated response.

nextToken.documentation: A pagination token from a previous paginated response indicating you wish to get the next page of results.

state.documentation: Filter the results to only include Connection objects in the given Connection$state.

environmentId.documentation: Filter the results to only include Connection objects on the given Environment.

provider.documentation: Filter the results to only include Connection objects to the given Provider.

attachPoint.documentation: Filter results to only include Connection objects attached to the given AttachPoint.

nextToken.documentation: A pagination token for use in subsequent calls to fetch the next page of results.

maxResults.documentation: The max number of list results in a single paginated response.

nextToken.documentation: A pagination token from a previous paginated response indicating you wish to get the next page of results.

provider.documentation: Filter results to only include Environment objects that connect to the Provider.

location.documentation: Filter results to only include Environment objects that connect to a given location distiguisher.

nextToken.documentation: A pagination token for use in subsequent calls to fetch the next page of results.

arn.documentation: The resource ARN for which to list tags.

cloudServiceProvider.documentation: The provider's name. Specifically, connections to/from this Cloud Service Provider will be considered Multicloud connections.

lastMileProvider.documentation: The provider's name. Specifically, connections to/from this Last Mile Provider will be considered LastMile connections.

Provider.documentation: Describes the respective AWS Interconnect Partner organization.

identifier.documentation: A generic bit of identifying information. Can be used in place of any of the more specific types.

RemoteAccountIdentifier.documentation: The types of identifiers that may be needed for remote account specification.

ResourceNotFoundException.documentation: The request specifies a resource that does not exist on the server.

ServiceQuotaExceededException.documentation: The requested operation would result in the calling principal exceeding their allotted quota.

arn.documentation: The ARN of the resource that should receive the new tags.

tags.documentation: A map of tags to apply to the specified resource.

ThrottlingException.documentation: The request was denied due to request throttling.

arn.documentation: The ARN of the resource from which the specified tags should be removed.

tagKeys.documentation: The list of tag keys that should be removed from the resource.

identifier.documentation: The identifier of the Connection that should be updated.

description.documentation: An updated description to apply to the Connection

bandwidth.documentation:
Request a new bandwidth size on the given Connection.

Note that changes to the size may be subject to additional policy, and does require the remote partner provider to acknowledge and permit this new bandwidth size.

documentation: Provides managed connections between your AWS network resources and select partner network resources.
